root_text.an4.53:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā antarā ca madhuraṁ antarā ca verañjaṁ addhānamaggappaṭipanno hoti.
root_text.an4.53:1.2: Sambahulāpi kho gahapatī ca gahapatāniyo ca antarā ca madhuraṁ antarā ca verañjaṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti.
root_text.an4.53:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā maggā okkamma aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle (…) nisīdi.
root_text.an4.53:1.4: Addasaṁsu kho gahapatī ca gahapatāniyo ca bhagavantaṁ aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle nisinnaṁ.
root_text.an4.53:1.5: Disvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te gahapatī ca gahapatāniyo ca bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an4.53:3.1: Kathañca, gahapatayo, chavo chavāya saddhiṁ saṁvasati?
root_text.an4.53:3.2: Idha, gahapatayo, sāmiko hoti pāṇātipātī adinnādāyī kāmesumicchācārī musāvādī surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhāyī dussīlo pāpadhammo maccheramalapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati akkosakaparibhāsako samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ;
root_text.an4.53:3.3: bhariyāpissa hoti pāṇātipātinī adinnādāyinī kāmesumicchācārinī musāvādinī surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhāyinī dussīlā pāpadhammā maccheramalapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati akkosikaparibhāsikā samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ.
root_text.an4.53:3.4: Evaṁ kho, gahapatayo, chavo chavāya saddhiṁ saṁvasati.
root_text.an4.53:4.1: Kathañca, gahapatayo, chavo deviyā saddhiṁ saṁvasati?
root_text.an4.53:4.2: Idha, gahapatayo, sāmiko hoti pāṇātipātī adinnādāyī kāmesumicchācārī musāvādī surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhāyī dussīlo pāpadhammo maccheramalapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati akkosakaparibhāsako samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ;
root_text.an4.53:4.3: bhariyā khvassa hoti pāṇātipātā paṭiviratā adinnādānā paṭiviratā kāmesumicchācārā paṭiviratā musāvādā paṭiviratā surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭiviratā sīlavatī kalyāṇadhammā vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati anakkosikaparibhāsikā samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ.
root_text.an4.53:4.4: Evaṁ kho, gahapatayo, chavo deviyā saddhiṁ saṁvasati.
root_text.an4.53:5.1: Kathañca, gahapatayo, devo chavāya saddhiṁ saṁvasati?
root_text.an4.53:5.2: Idha, gahapatayo, sāmiko hoti pāṇātipātā paṭivirato adinnādānā paṭivirato kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato musāvādā paṭivirato surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato sīlavā kalyāṇadhammo vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati anakkosakaparibhāsako samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ;
root_text.an4.53:5.3: bhariyā khvassa hoti pāṇātipātinī …pe… surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhāyinī dussīlā pāpadhammā maccheramalapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati akkosikaparibhāsikā samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ.
root_text.an4.53:5.4: Evaṁ kho, gahapatayo, devo chavāya saddhiṁ saṁvasati.
root_text.an4.53:6.1: Kathañca, gahapatayo, devo deviyā saddhiṁ saṁvasati?
root_text.an4.53:6.2: Idha, gahapatayo, sāmiko hoti pāṇātipātā paṭivirato …pe… sīlavā kalyāṇadhammo vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati anakkosakaparibhāsako samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ;
root_text.an4.53:6.3: bhariyāpissa hoti pāṇātipātā paṭiviratā …pe… surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭiviratā sīlavatī kalyāṇadhammā vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati anakkosikaparibhāsikā samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ.
root_text.an4.53:6.4: Evaṁ kho, gahapatayo, devo deviyā saddhiṁ saṁvasati.

translation_text.an4.53:1.1: At one time the Buddha was traveling along the road between Madhurā and Verañjā,
translation_text.an4.53:1.2: as were several householders, both women and men.
translation_text.an4.53:1.3: The Buddha left the road and sat at the root of a tree,
translation_text.an4.53:1.5: They went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to them:
translation_text.an4.53:2.1: “Householders, there are four ways of living together.
translation_text.an4.53:2.3: A male zombie living with a female zombie;
translation_text.an4.53:2.4: a male zombie living with a goddess;
translation_text.an4.53:2.5: a god living with a female zombie;
translation_text.an4.53:2.6: a god living with a goddess.
translation_text.an4.53:3.1: And how does a male zombie live with a female zombie?
translation_text.an4.53:3.2: It’s when the husband kills living creatures, steals, commits sexual misconduct, lies, and consumes beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants. He’s unethical, of bad character, living at home with his heart full of the stain of stinginess, abusing and insulting ascetics and brahmins.
translation_text.an4.53:3.3: And the wife is also … unethical, of bad character …
translation_text.an4.53:3.4: That’s how a male zombie lives with a female zombie.
translation_text.an4.53:4.1: And how does a male zombie live with a goddess?
translation_text.an4.53:4.2: It’s when the husband … is unethical, of bad character …
translation_text.an4.53:4.3: But the wife doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants. She’s ethical, of good character, living at home with her heart rid of the stain of stinginess, not abusing and insulting ascetics and brahmins.
translation_text.an4.53:4.4: That’s how a male zombie lives with a goddess.
translation_text.an4.53:5.1: And how does a god live with a female zombie?
translation_text.an4.53:5.2: It’s when the husband … is ethical, of good character …
translation_text.an4.53:5.3: But the wife … is unethical, of bad character …
translation_text.an4.53:5.4: That’s how a god lives with a female zombie.
translation_text.an4.53:6.1: And how does a god live with a goddess?
translation_text.an4.53:6.2: It’s when the husband … is ethical, of good character …
translation_text.an4.53:6.3: And the wife is also … ethical, of good character …
translation_text.an4.53:6.4: That’s how a god lives with a goddess.
translation_text.an4.53:6.5: These are the four ways of living together.
translation_text.an4.53:8.6: with a zombie for a husband.
translation_text.an4.53:9.6: with a god for a husband.
translation_text.an4.53:10.1: When both are faithful and bountiful,
translation_text.an4.53:10.4: say nice things to each other.
translation_text.an4.53:11.4: when both are equal in ethics.
translation_text.an4.53:12.2: both equal in precepts and observances,
translation_text.an4.53:12.3: they delight in the heavenly realm,
translation_text.an4.53:12.4: enjoying all the pleasures they desire.”

variant_text.an4.53:11.1: Atthāsaṁ pacurā → atthā sampacurā (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
